# SC-11: Container & IaC Security Scanning

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** SHOULD (recommended) · **Status:** draft

Scan all container images, Dockerfiles, Kubernetes manifests, Terraform configurations, Helm charts, and other Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) artefacts at commit time for: known vulnerabilities (CVE) in base images and layers, CIS benchmark violations, security misconfigurations, and hardcoded secrets in manifests. Enforce policies: no base images with critical CVEs, no privileged containers without justification, no host network access without explicit approval, no default service account permissions, secrets not passed as environment variables. Block commits that violate policies.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 3 | Coding & Implementation | Mandatory | *Container and IaC security scanning at Stage 3 shifts security left before artefacts reach testing. SC-16 checks production baseline; this control prevents IaC misconfigurations from being committed.*

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Automated scanning with human policy exception management |
| **Agent does** | Execute container and IaC security scanner (Trivy, Snyk, Checkov, or equivalent) against all container images, Dockerfiles, and IaC manifests. Check for: CVEs in base images, CIS benchmark compliance, security misconfigurations, hardcoded secrets. Reject commits violating security policies. Flag findings for human review. |
| **Human does** | Review flagged issues. Decide: remediate (update base image, fix misconfiguration), accept risk and document exception (with business justification), or redesign to remove the risky configuration. |

## Inputs

| Artifact | Source Control | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| Container images and Dockerfiles | All Dockerfile and container image source code |  |
| IaC manifests | Kubernetes YAML, Terraform, Helm charts, CloudFormation, or equivalent |  |
| CVE database and CIS benchmarks | Current vulnerability intelligence and infrastructure security standards |  |


## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Container & IaC Security Scan Report | report | Findings by category: CVE vulnerabilities in base images, CIS benchmark violations, security misconfigurations, hardcoded secrets. Include affected files, severity, and remediation guidance. | 7 years (audit trail) |
| Exception tickets (if any) | decision | Documented exceptions for accepted IaC security issues, including business justification and approval sign-off | 7 years |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | 15 min |
| **Tooling** | Trivy, Snyk Container Security, Checkov (IaC scanning), Aqua Security, HashiCorp Sentinel (Terraform policy) |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Infrastructure Security Engineer |

Critical container or IaC issues block commit unless an exception ticket is linked. Examples: critical-severity CVE in base image, privileged container without justification, hardcoded secrets in manifests.

## Dependencies

- `SC-08`
- `SC-10`


## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 9(2) | Protection of ICT infrastructure including containerised workloads |
| DORA | Art. 25 | Vulnerability assessments must include infrastructure components |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(4) | Appropriate cybersecurity in production environment for AI systems deployed in containers |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security, Infrastructure · **Owner role:** Infrastructure Security Engineer
